learning_outcomes.description: communicate effectively and professionally with clients, supervisors, staff and work teams
identify, define, analyse and implement strategies to combat issues of motivation and adherence for clients in the exercise setting
identify career options or clinical contexts for practice in exercise science
be able to apply foundation skills and competencies of exercise science in a professional context
appreciate cultural, legal and ethical issues in the development and management of exercise programs and business plans
develop interprofessional practice skills including role clarification, team functioning, interprofessional communication and client-centred care.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment item contributes up to 10% of your total grade.

It is an in-class problem-based learning (PBL) task. Inspera Assessment will be used for the PBL task: it will be a invigilated on-campus assessment which you will complete using your own device. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided in the Blackboard site for this course. The PBL activity will include the following components:

  • Ethics
  • Scope of practice
  • Telehealth
  • Exercise implications of medications
  • Risk assessment
  • Duty of care
  • Insurance
  • Confidentiality
  • Cultural & Linguistic Diversity
  • Cultural safety*

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students interactions with their client over the course of the training period will be assessed by their allocated supervisor. Students are assessed based on their ability to conduct an initial and final assessment demonstrating their technical skills and competencies and their ability to design and deliver a safe, effective and client-centred exercise intervention, including telehealth sessions. Students will receive verbal feedback from their supervisor on a weekly basis and then a final mark at the end of the semester. Only the final mark is counted.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Clinical Educators directly assess student performance in real-time and submit directly to the Course Coordinator.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Draft and Final Personal Training Report
assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 9/11/2026

Draft Program: due 1 (one) week following the client's initial assessment.

SOAP notes, record of activities, programs and reflections: due within 48 hours of each personal training and group training session completed.

Final Report: 1:00pm on 9/11/2026.
assessments.task_description:
This is the capstone assessment piece for this course. It comprises the following main tasks:

Task 1: After assessing your client, and prior to seeing your client for a second time, you are required to submit a draft program (incl. summary of results), which will be assessed by your Clinical Educator. You will receive feedback which must be implemented prior to seeing your client for your first session. The due date is Week 3, exactly 7 days after completing the initial assessment (e.g., if assessment is completed Monday of week 2, Draft Program is due Monday week 3). The Draft Program will contribute to your Final Report and must be included as an appendix in your Final Report submission.

Task 2: SOAP notes, record of activities, programs and reflections need to be uploaded to Nookal within 48 hours of each personal training and group training session completed. These must be included as an appendix in your Final Report submission where indicated.

Task 3: Supervisor reference forms and ESSA-related documents must be signed and completed and included within your Final Report submission.

Task 4: Submit a Final Report for your supervisor and a Brief Report for your client. You must use the template provided and follow the task description and marking criteria sheet, which will be made available via Blackboard.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: Check due dates on HMBS1240 Learn.UQ site for this module. Assessment due 2 weeks after last allocated tutorial.
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.category: Essay/ Critique, Participation/ Student contribution, Presentation, Project
assessments.task_description:
Please refer to the assessment information on Learn.UQ for the assessment due date for your team.

This is a team assessment, and all team members are expected to contribute equally to the team’s assessment items. Teams will be assigned during your first tutorial. There are three (3) parts to this assessment:

Part One requires you to complete a worksheet and will draw on content from Module 1 of the IPCP curriculum focusing on role-clarification and negotiation, and relationship focused care and services. Length: There is no word limit for the worksheet.

Part Two involves creating a 3-minute video which draws upon the content from Modules 2 and 3 of the IPCP curriculum focusing on team communication, team functioning and relationship focused care and services. Length: Video 3 minutes.

Part Three involves a check-in with a tutor, to assess and reflect on processes of collaboration

Length: approx. 10 minutes. 

Teams will need to submit Parts One and Two of your assessment in the Assessment tab on the HMBS1240 IPCP Learn.UQ course site. Additional information regarding submission will be provided on the HMBS1240 IPCP Learn.UQ course site.

Please refer to the assessment information on Learn.UQ for further details and requirements for the assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Personal Training of Client

Students interactions with their client over the course of the training period will be assessed by their allocated supervisor. Students are assessed

based on their ability to conduct an initial and final assessment demonstrating their technical skills and competencies and their ability to design and

deliver a safe, effective and client-centred exercise intervention. Students will receive verbal feedback from their supervisor on a weekly basis and then

a final mark at the end of the semester. Only the final mark is counted.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

Students are assessed based on their ability to conduct an initial and final assessment demonstrating their technical skills and competencies and their ability to design and deliver a safe, effective and client-centred exercise intervention. Students will receive verbal feedback from their supervisor on a weekly basis and then a final mark at the end of the semester. Only the final mark is counted.

learning_activities.topic:
Draft and Final Reports

After assessing your client, and prior to seeing your client for a second time, you are required to submit a draft program (incl. summary of

results), which will be assessed by your session supervisor. In addition to your mark (out of 20), you will receive feedback which must be implemented prior to seeing your client for your first session.

At the end of the 8-week personal training block, each student is required to submit a final report for their personal training client. They are required to use the Template provided and follow the task description and marking criteria sheet, which will be made available via Blackboard.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Draft and Final Reports After assessing your client, and prior to seeing your client for a second time, you are required to submit a draft program (incl. summary of results), which will be assessed by your session supervisor. In addition to your mark (out of 20), you will receive feedback which must be implemented prior to seeing your client for your first session. At the end of the 8-week personal training block, each student is required to submit a final report for their personal training client. They are required to use the Template provided and follow the task description and marking criteria sheet, which will be made available via Blackboard.

learning_activities.topic:
Problem Based Learning

There are 3 activities within the learning activity. Activity/section 1 relates to HMST3001 (your first placement with ‘apparently healthy populations’ and activity/section 2 relates to HMST4314 (exercise science major practicum) or EXMD4701/4702 (clinical exercise physiology practicum). The task aims, descriptions,

and marking rubrics for each activity are outlined on Blackboard. Industry representatives will give lectures throughout semester that will inform you of possible prac placement options.

Activity 3 relates to the Problem Base Learning (PBL) activities will be covered in lectures throughout semester and will cover:

  • Ethics
  • Scope of practice
  • Exercise implications of medications
  • Risk assessment
  • Duty of care
  • Insurance
  • Confidentiality
  • Cultural & Linguistic Diversity
  • Cultural safety – Indigenous people


Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Interprofessional Collaborative Practice Module

Interprofessional Collaborative Practice Modules (Monday pm/ Thursday am) (Tutorials); Dates on Allocate +

Participants will work in small teams in tutorial groups with students from Dentistry, Exercise Physiology and Sports Science, Midwifery, Nursing, Occupational Therapy, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Social Work and Speech Pathology.

There are three modules that consist of on-line preparation and face-to-face learning activities. You will work in your team to engage in a range of learning activities to develop your knowledge and skills to help prepare you for working in teams when caring for clients.

·     In Module One, you will learn about relationship-focused care, role clarification and negotiation in the interprofessional context. You will explore trust and how to build trust and respect for colleagues and other health professionals, the person(s) receiving care, and their care team.

·     In Module Two you will develop the skills to communicate effectively with team members and appreciate the consequences when communication breaks down and is ineffective. This includes the role of communication in patient safety, handovers and transitions of care, health literacy, and communicating with diverse patient and population groups.

·     In Module Three, you explore team functioning, including barriers and enablers, consumer perspectives on team functioning in healthcare, and the psychosocial hazards which impact team functioning.

You will also follow the healthcare journey of a client across the three modules.


Learning outcomes: L06
